kern/syscall/write_syscall.c

Go to the documentation of this file.
00001 /*
00002  * write_syscall.c
00003  *
00004  *  Created on: Apr 16, 2010
00005  *      Author: Luigi Grimaudo
00006  *                      Piera Gueli
00007  */
00008 
00009 #include <types.h>
00010 #include <kern/errno.h>
00011 #include <kern/reboot.h>
00012 #include <kern/unistd.h>
00013 #include <lib.h>
00014 #include <spl.h>
00015 #include <clock.h>
00016 #include <thread.h>
00017 #include <current.h>
00018 #include <synch.h>
00019 #include <vm.h>
00020 #include <vfs.h>
00021 #include <mainbus.h>
00022 #include <vfs.h>
00023 #include <vnode.h>
00024 #include <device.h>
00025 #include <syscall.h>
00026 #include <syscall.h>
00027 #include <kern/fcntl.h>
00028 #include <kern/file.h>
00029 
00030 /*
00031  * Write writes up to nbytes bytes to the file specified by fd, at the location
00032  * in the file specified by the current seek position of the file, taking the
00033  * data from the space pointed to by buf. The file must be open for writing.
00034  * The current seek position of the file is advanced by the number of bytes
00035  * written. Each write (or read) operation is atomic relative to other I/O to
00036  * the same file.
00037  *
00038  */
00039 
00040 int sys_write(int fd, const void* buf, size_t nbytes, int* retval)
00041 {
00042         struct file* fcb;
00043         struct uio u;
00044         struct iovec iov;
00045         int errno;
00046         int spl;
00047 
00048         /* Check if buf is valid */
00049         if (!buf) {
00050                 
00051                 errno = EFAULT;
00052                 return errno;
00053         }
00054 
00055         /* Get fcb using fd as index of the open files table */
00056         fcb = curthread->table_fd[fd];
00057 
00058         /* Check if the open files table entry is valid */
00059         if ( fcb == NULL ) 
00060         {               
00061                 errno = EBADF;
00062                 return errno;
00063         }
00064 
00065         /* Check if the file is opened in writing mode */
00066         if ( fcb->writeable != true ) {
00067                 errno = EBADF;
00068                 return errno;
00069         }
00070 
00071         /* Check if byte to write is more than one */
00072         if( nbytes < 1 ){
00073                 *retval = 0;
00074                 return 0;
00075         }
00076 
00077 
00078         iov.iov_ubase = (userptr_t)buf;         /* Location of the buffer */
00079         iov.iov_len = nbytes;                           /* Lenght of the buffer */
00080 
00081         /* Set up the uio for writing */
00082         u.uio_iov = &iov;
00083         u.uio_iovcnt = 1;
00084         u.uio_resid = nbytes;                   /* Amount of data to transfer */
00085         u.uio_offset = fcb->offset;
00086         u.uio_segflg = UIO_USERISPACE;
00087         u.uio_rw = UIO_WRITE;
00088         u.uio_space = curthread->t_addrspace;
00089 
00090         /* Make the write atomic for the file */
00091         spl = splhigh();
00092 
00093         /* Try to write the file */
00094         *retval = VOP_WRITE(fcb->vfs_node, &u);
00095 
00096         splx(spl);
00097 
00098         /* Check write result */
00099         if ( (*retval) ) {
00100                 errno = EIO;
00101                 return errno;
00102         }
00103 
00104         /* Return the number of bytes written to user */
00105         *retval = nbytes - u.uio_resid;
00106 
00107         /* Update seek position */
00108         fcb->offset += *retval;
00109 
00110         return 0;
00111 }
Generated on Sun Jun 27 22:12:13 2010 for Os/161 by  1.6.1
